title: Market Brief — Sunday, March 29, 2026
summary: GPU availability sits at 29% for H100 (84/286), 28% for H200 (113/403), and 30% for B200 (98/327), all in a narrow band signaling a balanced but not loose market. The headline move is B200 spot crashing 18.5% day-over-day to $7.49/hr, suggesting early oversupply in next-gen silicon while legacy iron holds firm with H100 ticking up 4.1%.
paragraphs: GPU availability sits at 29% for H100 (84/286), 28% for H200 (113/403), and 30% for B200 (98/327), all in a narrow band signaling a balanced but not loose market. The headline move is B200 spot crashing 18.5% day-over-day to $7.49/hr, suggesting early oversupply in next-gen silicon while legacy iron holds firm with H100 ticking up 4.1%.

At TCO-adjusted cost (1.25x spot), Llama 70B inference runs roughly $6.09/M on H100, $5.56/M on H200, and $6.84/M on B200. Medium-tier API sellers are bleeding everywhere: at $3/M output, they lose roughly $3.09/M tokens on H100. DeepSeek V3.2 at $0.38/M output is burning cash hardest. Provider grades: OpenAI Strong, Anthropic Neutral, Google Neutral, DeepSeek Burning Cash.

OpenAI o3 Pro at $80/M output is overvalued given GPT-5.4 benchmarks at $15/M, a 5x premium that reasoning-intensive use cases alone cannot justify. B200 spot at $7.49/hr after an 18.5% single-day drop is the undervalued trade: lock in contracts now before availability tightens below the current 30%.
bullets: 29% of H100 GPUs are available, with just 84 of 286 units free on the spot market.
30% B200 availability at 98 of 327 units shows no scarcity despite being the newest class.
$7.49/hr B200 median spot is down 18.5% in one day, the largest single-session GPU price drop in this dataset.
$0.38/M output from DeepSeek V3.2 implies a per-token loss of approximately $5.71/M against TCO-adjusted H100 cost.
21.41 on the LLMflation Index means token prices have fallen roughly 79% since GPT-4 launched at base 100.
$80/M output for o3 Pro is 211x more expensive than DeepSeek V3.2 at $0.38/M, the widest spread across all 18 tracked models.
